{{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|50|0.55|N|135|40|8.7|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Makino Kurumazuka Kofun'''|牧野車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Ogura Higashimachi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Hirakata, Osaka|Hirakata, Osaka]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922 with the area under protection expanded in 1980.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191212 |title=牧野車塚古墳 |trans-title=Makino Kurumazuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Makino Kurumazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located an elevation of 22 meters, on the northern edge of the Hirakata Plateau formed on the left bank of the [[:en:Yodo River|Yodo River]]. The main axis is in the east-west direction parallel to the terrain with the front orientated to the east. It has a total length of 107.5 meters with a posterior circular portion diameter of 54.5 meters. It is surrounded by an empty moat with a width of ten meters, of which there are remnants of the outer embankment on the west and south sides the tumulus. The interior of the tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of its internal structure, and the presence of any [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] is unknown. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found on the surface of the tumulus. The ''fukiishi'' are unique in that stone slabs made from stone from [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]] and [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]s are used, rather than local river rocks as is common at most other sites. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From the shape of the tumulus and the types of ''haniwa'' found in the vicinity, it is estimated to date from the middleof the Kofun period, or the late 4th century. To the southwest of this tumulus were once several smaller ''[[kofun]]'', with names such as Akatsuka, Gongenzuka, Kodozuka, and Shogazuka, but all of these tumuli have been destroyed by urban encroachment.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Currently, the Makino Kurumazuka Kofun is being maintained as part of "Kurumazuka Park". It is located about a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Makino Station (Osaka)|Makino Station]] on the [[:en:Keihan Main Line|Keihan Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 107.5 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 44 meters wide, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 54.5 meter diameter, 2-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Makino Kurumazuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the posterior portion File:Makino Kurumazuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|Anterior looking towards Posterior File:Makino Kurumazuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|Posterior looking towards anterior File:Makino Kurumazuka Kofun, hori.jpg|Remnant of moat </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]

= [[:en:Hirakata, Osaka|Hirakata, Osaka]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|50|0.55|N|135|40|8.7|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Makino Kurumazuka Kofun'''|牧野車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun}}, located in the Ogura Higashimachi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Hirakata, Osaka|Hirakata, Osaka]] in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922 with the area under protection expanded in 1980.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191212 |title=牧野車塚古墳 |trans-title=Makino Kurumazuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Makino Kurumazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located an elevation of 22 meters, on the northern edge of the Hirakata Plateau formed on the left bank of the {{ill|Yodo River|en|Yodo River|WD=Q1140505|lt=Yodo River}}. The main axis is in the east-west direction parallel to the terrain with the front orientated to the east. It has a total length of 107.5 meters with a posterior circular portion diameter of 54.5 meters. It is surrounded by an empty moat with a width of ten meters, of which there are remnants of the outer embankment on the west and south sides the tumulus. The interior of the tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of its internal structure, and the presence of any {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} is unknown. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been found on the surface of the tumulus. The ''fukiishi'' are unique in that stone slabs made from stone from {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|WD=Q160734|lt=Tokushima}} and {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}s are used, rather than local river rocks as is common at most other sites. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From the shape of the tumulus and the types of ''haniwa'' found in the vicinity, it is estimated to date from the middleof the Kofun period, or the late 4th century. To the southwest of this tumulus were once several smaller ''[[kofun]]'', with names such as Akatsuka, Gongenzuka, Kodozuka, and Shogazuka, but all of these tumuli have been destroyed by urban encroachment.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Currently, the Makino Kurumazuka Kofun is being maintained as part of "Kurumazuka Park". It is located about a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Makino Station (Osaka)|en|Makino Station (Osaka)|WD=Q1825108|lt=Makino Station}} on the {{ill|Keihan Main Line|en|Keihan Main Line|WD=Q2598752|lt=Keihan Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 107.5 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 44 meters wide, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 54.5 meter diameter, 2-tiers

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Hirakata, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} | name = Makino Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =牧野車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Makino Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Makino Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Makino Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.833417|N|135.668917|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Hirakata, Osaka|Hirakata, Osaka]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|50|0.55|N|135|40|8.7|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Makino Kurumazuka Kofun'''|牧野車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun}}, located in the Ogura Higashimachi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Hirakata, Osaka|Hirakata, Osaka]] in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922 with the area under protection expanded in 1980.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191212 |title=牧野車塚古墳 |trans-title=Makino Kurumazuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Makino Kurumazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located an elevation of 22 meters, on the northern edge of the Hirakata Plateau formed on the left bank of the {{ill|Yodo River|en|Yodo River|WD=Q1140505|lt=Yodo River}}. The main axis is in the east-west direction parallel to the terrain with the front orientated to the east. It has a total length of 107.5 meters with a posterior circular portion diameter of 54.5 meters. It is surrounded by an empty moat with a width of ten meters, of which there are remnants of the outer embankment on the west and south sides the tumulus. The interior of the tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of its internal structure, and the presence of any {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} is unknown. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been found on the surface of the tumulus. The ''fukiishi'' are unique in that stone slabs made from stone from {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|WD=Q160734|lt=Tokushima}} and {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}s are used, rather than local river rocks as is common at most other sites. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From the shape of the tumulus and the types of ''haniwa'' found in the vicinity, it is estimated to date from the middleof the Kofun period, or the late 4th century. To the southwest of this tumulus were once several smaller ''[[kofun]]'', with names such as Akatsuka, Gongenzuka, Kodozuka, and Shogazuka, but all of these tumuli have been destroyed by urban encroachment.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Currently, the Makino Kurumazuka Kofun is being maintained as part of "Kurumazuka Park". It is located about a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Makino Station (Osaka)|en|Makino Station (Osaka)|WD=Q1825108|lt=Makino Station}} on the {{ill|Keihan Main Line|en|Keihan Main Line|WD=Q2598752|lt=Keihan Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 107.5 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 44 meters wide, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 54.5 meter diameter, 2-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Makino Kurumazuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the posterior portion File:Makino Kurumazuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|Anterior looking towards Posterior File:Makino Kurumazuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|Posterior looking towards anterior File:Makino Kurumazuka Kofun, hori.jpg|Remnant of moat </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Makino Kurumazuka Kofun}} *[https://www.city.hirakata.osaka.jp/0000035749.html Hirakata City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://osaka-info.jp/spot/masano-kurumazuka-tumulus/ Osaka Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/maibun/oldpicture_makinokur.html Osaka Prefectural Government] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Hirakata, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | name = Makino Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =牧野車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Makino Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Makino Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Makino Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.833417|N|135.668917|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Hirakata, Osaka|Hirakata, Osaka]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|50|0.55|N|135|40|8.7|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Makino Kurumazuka Kofun'''|牧野車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, located in the Ogura Higashimachi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Hirakata, Osaka|Hirakata, Osaka]] in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922 with the area under protection expanded in 1980.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191212 |title=牧野車塚古墳 |trans-title=Makino Kurumazuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Makino Kurumazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located an elevation of 22 meters, on the northern edge of the Hirakata Plateau formed on the left bank of the {{ill|Yodo River|en|Yodo River|WD=Q1140505|lt=Yodo River|1=Yodo River}}. The main axis is in the east-west direction parallel to the terrain with the front orientated to the east. It has a total length of 107.5 meters with a posterior circular portion diameter of 54.5 meters. It is surrounded by an empty moat with a width of ten meters, of which there are remnants of the outer embankment on the west and south sides the tumulus. The interior of the tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of its internal structure, and the presence of any {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} is unknown. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found on the surface of the tumulus. The ''fukiishi'' are unique in that stone slabs made from stone from {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|WD=Q160734|lt=Tokushima|1=Tokushima Prefecture}} and {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}s are used, rather than local river rocks as is common at most other sites. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From the shape of the tumulus and the types of ''haniwa'' found in the vicinity, it is estimated to date from the middleof the Kofun period, or the late 4th century. To the southwest of this tumulus were once several smaller ''[[kofun]]'', with names such as Akatsuka, Gongenzuka, Kodozuka, and Shogazuka, but all of these tumuli have been destroyed by urban encroachment.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Currently, the Makino Kurumazuka Kofun is being maintained as part of "Kurumazuka Park". It is located about a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Makino Station (Osaka)|en|Makino Station (Osaka)|WD=Q1825108|lt=Makino Station|1=Makino Station (Osaka)}} on the {{ill|Keihan Main Line|en|Keihan Main Line|WD=Q2598752|lt=Keihan Main Line|1=Keihan Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 107.5 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 44 meters wide, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 54.5 meter diameter, 2-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Makino Kurumazuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the posterior portion File:Makino Kurumazuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|Anterior looking towards Posterior File:Makino Kurumazuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|Posterior looking towards anterior File:Makino Kurumazuka Kofun, hori.jpg|Remnant of moat </gallery>

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Hirakata, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | name = Makino Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =牧野車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Makino Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Makino Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Makino Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.833417|N|135.668917|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Hirakata, Osaka|Hirakata, Osaka]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|50|0.55|N|135|40|8.7|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Makino Kurumazuka Kofun'''|牧野車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, located in the Ogura Higashimachi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Hirakata, Osaka|Hirakata, Osaka]] in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922 with the area under protection expanded in 1980.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191212 |title=牧野車塚古墳 |trans-title=Makino Kurumazuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Makino Kurumazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located an elevation of 22 meters, on the northern edge of the Hirakata Plateau formed on the left bank of the {{ill|Yodo River|en|Yodo River|WD=Q1140505|lt=Yodo River|1=Yodo River}}. The main axis is in the east-west direction parallel to the terrain with the front orientated to the east. It has a total length of 107.5 meters with a posterior circular portion diameter of 54.5 meters. It is surrounded by an empty moat with a width of ten meters, of which there are remnants of the outer embankment on the west and south sides the tumulus. The interior of the tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of its internal structure, and the presence of any {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} is unknown. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found on the surface of the tumulus. The ''fukiishi'' are unique in that stone slabs made from stone from {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|WD=Q160734|lt=Tokushima|1=Tokushima Prefecture}} and {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}s are used, rather than local river rocks as is common at most other sites. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From the shape of the tumulus and the types of ''haniwa'' found in the vicinity, it is estimated to date from the middleof the Kofun period, or the late 4th century. To the southwest of this tumulus were once several smaller ''[[kofun]]'', with names such as Akatsuka, Gongenzuka, Kodozuka, and Shogazuka, but all of these tumuli have been destroyed by urban encroachment.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Currently, the Makino Kurumazuka Kofun is being maintained as part of "Kurumazuka Park". It is located about a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Makino Station (Osaka)|en|Makino Station (Osaka)|WD=Q1825108|lt=Makino Station|1=Makino Station (Osaka)}} on the {{ill|Keihan Main Line|en|Keihan Main Line|WD=Q2598752|lt=Keihan Main Line|1=Keihan Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 107.5 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 44 meters wide, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 54.5 meter diameter, 2-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Makino Kurumazuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the posterior portion File:Makino Kurumazuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|Anterior looking towards Posterior File:Makino Kurumazuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|Posterior looking towards anterior File:Makino Kurumazuka Kofun, hori.jpg|Remnant of moat </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Makino Kurumazuka Kofun}} *[https://www.city.hirakata.osaka.jp/0000035749.html Hirakata City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://osaka-info.jp/spot/masano-kurumazuka-tumulus/ Osaka Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/maibun/oldpicture_makinokur.html Osaka Prefectural Government] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Hirakata, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | name = Makino Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =牧野車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Makino Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Makino Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Makino Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.833417|N|135.668917|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Hirakata, Osaka|Hirakata, Osaka]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|50|0.55|N|135|40|8.7|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Makino Kurumazuka Kofun'''|牧野車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, located in the Ogura Higashimachi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Hirakata, Osaka|Hirakata, Osaka]] in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922 with the area under protection expanded in 1980.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191212 |title=牧野車塚古墳 |trans-title=Makino Kurumazuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Makino Kurumazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun|1=[[Zenpōkōenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located an elevation of 22 meters, on the northern edge of the Hirakata Plateau formed on the left bank of the {{ill|Yodo River|en|Yodo River|qid=Q1140505|lt=Yodo River|1=Yodo River}}. The main axis is in the east-west direction parallel to the terrain with the front orientated to the east. It has a total length of 107.5 meters with a posterior circular portion diameter of 54.5 meters. It is surrounded by an empty moat with a width of ten meters, of which there are remnants of the outer embankment on the west and south sides the tumulus. The interior of the tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of its internal structure, and the presence of any {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} is unknown. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found on the surface of the tumulus. The ''fukiishi'' are unique in that stone slabs made from stone from {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|qid=Q160734|lt=Tokushima|1=Tokushima Prefecture}} and {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}s are used, rather than local river rocks as is common at most other sites. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From the shape of the tumulus and the types of ''haniwa'' found in the vicinity, it is estimated to date from the middleof the Kofun period, or the late 4th century. To the southwest of this tumulus were once several smaller ''[[kofun]]'', with names such as Akatsuka, Gongenzuka, Kodozuka, and Shogazuka, but all of these tumuli have been destroyed by urban encroachment.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Currently, the Makino Kurumazuka Kofun is being maintained as part of "Kurumazuka Park". It is located about a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Makino Station (Osaka)|en|Makino Station (Osaka)|qid=Q1825108|lt=Makino Station|1=Makino Station (Osaka)}} on the {{ill|Keihan Main Line|en|Keihan Main Line|qid=Q2598752|lt=Keihan Main Line|1=Keihan Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 107.5 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 44 meters wide, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 54.5 meter diameter, 2-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Makino Kurumazuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the posterior portion File:Makino Kurumazuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|Anterior looking towards Posterior File:Makino Kurumazuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|Posterior looking towards anterior File:Makino Kurumazuka Kofun, hori.jpg|Remnant of moat </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
